content.text:
OK — so there's a `.git-rewrite` directory lingering (leftover from a `git filter-branch` or similar rewrite operation), and a cron agent is repeatedly trying to clean it up but running into edit failures because it can't match the exact text in `.gitignore`. The `.git-rewrite/` entry is *already* in `.gitignore` now, but the directory itself is still being tracked/modified in the working tree.

This is a self-inflicting loop — a worker or cron job keeps touching `.git-rewrite/`, which generates modified files, which triggers more cleanup attempts. Let me just fix it.

arguments.command: cd /Users/landonking/openclaw-army && git rm -r --cached .git-rewrite/ 2>&1 && git checkout -- .git-rewrite/ 2>/dev/null; echo "--- checking if .git-rewrite is in index ---" && git ls-files .git-rewrite/ | head -5
